mission.title: flux: Flux CLI unable to retrieve image ressource when image CRD is not installed
mission.description: Flux CLI unable to retrieve image ressource when image CRD is not installed. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify flux troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flux deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n flux --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review flux configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux
```
### Describe the bug

Hi !

When I list all resources of flux using `flux get all`, got this error message:
```bash
✗ failed to get API group resources: unable to retrieve the complete list of server APIs: image.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1beta2: the server

steps.title: Apply the fix for Flux CLI unable to retrieve image ressource when image CRD
steps.description:
Whenever an API type is not available then both, `flux get all` and `flux get image all` will just skip over that type instead of logging an error message.

Before:

```
$ flux get all
✗ failed to get API group resources: unable to retrieve the complete list of server APIs:
```yaml
$ flux get all
✗ failed to get API group resources: unable to retrieve the complete list of server APIs: source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1beta2: the server could not find the requested resource
[...]
✗ failed to get API group resources: unable to retrieve the complete list of server APIs: image.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1beta2: the server could not find the requested resource
✗ failed to get API group resources: unable to retrieve the complete list of server APIs: image.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1beta2: the server could not find the requested resource
[...]
$ echo $?
0
```

steps.title: Confirm Flux CLI unable to retrieve image ressource when is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n flux --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Whenever an API type is not available then both, `flux get all` and `flux get image all` will just skip over that type instead of logging an error message.
codeSnippets:
$ flux get all
✗ failed to get API group resources: unable to retrieve the complete list of server APIs: source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1beta2: the server could not find the requested resource
[...]
✗ failed to get API group resources: unable to retrieve the complete list of server APIs: image.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1beta2: the server could not find the requested resource
✗ failed to get API group resources: unable to retrieve the complete list of server APIs: image.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1beta2: the server could not find the requested resource
[...]
$ echo $?
0

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
